agent_output: {"escalations":[{"type":"missing_input","description":"Pippen has not submitted any output. Both Feature A and Feature B share a single AuditLogService that MJ left without a defined interface or storage backend. This is exactly the integration seam Pippen needs to validate — shared service contract, concurrent write behavior, latency guarantees (the 1-second SLA from Bird's acceptance criteria), and failure modes if the audit log is unavailable during refund or suspension flows. Producing a handoff brief without Pippen's integration analysis risks Shaq building the AuditLogService with unstated assumptions that break under load or partial failure.","agents_involved":["Pippen"],"options":["Wait for Pippen to complete integration analysis on the shared AuditLogService before producing the handoff brief.","Ask MJ to fully specify the AuditLogService interface and storage backend first, then have Pippen validate, then synthesize.","Proceed without Pippen but flag the AuditLogService integration as an explicit open risk for Shaq — NOT recommended, as the shared service is the highest-risk component in this sprint."],"recommendation":"Wait for Pippen. The shared AuditLogService is the single point of coupling between both features. Without integration analysis covering its contract, storage backend, write concurrency model, and failure semantics, Shaq will have to guess — and guesses in shared infrastructure become production incidents. Ask Coach K to prioritize Pippen's review of the AuditLogService integration surface."}],"confidence":{"level":40,"high_confidence_areas":["Bird's domain rules for both features are clear and non-contradictory — both need audit logging with actor ID, timestamp, and a domain-specific payload (amount vs reason).","MJ's architecture correctly identifies a shared AuditLogService as the common dependency, which aligns with Bird's parallel audit requirements."],"low_confidence_areas":["AuditLogService interface — MJ references it in both features but has not defined its method signature, storage backend, or consistency guarantees. Cannot hand off to Shaq without this.","Integration behavior — concurrent audit writes from RefundService and AccountService hitting the same service; failure isolation if audit log is down; whether the 1-second SLA is measured end-to-end or just write latency. All Pippen's domain.","Whether AuditLogService.record() is synchronous or async, and what happens to the parent operation (refund/suspension) if the audit write fails — Bird's rules imply the audit entry is mandatory, which suggests the parent operation should fail too, but this is unstated."],"assumptions":["Bird intends the audit log entries for both features to live in the same logical audit store, given identical SLA requirements.","MJ intends a single shared AuditLogService implementation, not two feature-specific ones — inferred from the shared name but not explicitly confirmed.","The 1-second acceptance criterion refers to end-to-end visibility (write + query), not just write acknowledgment."]}}
